name: pending-stub
description: HA gate script (with documented pipefail fix), monorepo restructure skeleton scripts, chaos PDB test artifact, ArgoCD domain injection, and code-server workspace PVC. Advances the kind-cluster delivery chain and hardens toolchain autarky.
sprintGoal: Deliver a working HA gate script, advance monorepo restructure toward kind cluster, close ArgoCD domain injection gap, and add code-server workspace persistence.

stories.title: HA gate script validates all platform charts have replicaCount>=2, PDB, and podAntiAffinity
stories.description: Create scripts/ha-gate.sh that iterates every chart in charts/ and checks: (1) values.yaml has replicaCount >= 2, (2) helm template output contains PodDisruptionBudget, (3) helm template output contains podAntiAffinity. Script exits non-zero and prints FAIL:<chart>:<reason> for each violation. Print PASS:<chart> for each chart that passes all checks. Script must pass shellcheck -S error and support --dry-run flag that lists charts without running helm. IMPORTANT FIX (from sprint-23 review): The grep pipeline for extracting replicaCount must use '|| true' to prevent set -euo pipefail from killing the script when processing charts that have no top-level replicaCount field (e.g. platform/charts/_globals/). Pattern: replica_count=$(grep -E '^replicaCount:' "$chart/values.yaml" || true | awk ...)
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ha-gate.sh exists and passes shellcheck -S error: shellcheck -S error scripts/ha-gate.sh exits 0
scripts/ha-gate.sh --dry-run prints the list of charts it will check with output containing each chart directory name, then exits 0
For a chart that has replicaCount: 1 in values.yaml, scripts/ha-gate.sh outputs FAIL:<chart>:replicaCount < 2 and exits non-zero
For a chart with replicaCount>=2, PDB, and podAntiAffinity in templates, scripts/ha-gate.sh outputs PASS:<chart> for that chart

scripts/ha-gate.sh runs against all existing platform/charts/ without crashing on charts that lack a top-level replicaCount field (e.g. _globals): bash scripts/ha-gate.sh --dry-run 2>&1 | grep -v 'replicaCount' exits 0 for every chart listed
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error scripts/ha-gate.sh && bash -n scripts/ha-gate.sh && scripts/ha-gate.sh --dry-run; show all output verbatim. Then create a minimal test chart with replicaCount: 1 and run scripts/ha-gate.sh against it to confirm FAIL output. ALSO run scripts/ha-gate.sh against all existing platform/charts/ to confirm no crash on charts missing replicaCount.

smart.notes: Achievable score 4: three per-chart checks plus --dry-run flag plus pipefail guard is near the top of one-iteration budget.

stories.returnedReason: bash set -euo pipefail kills script on grep no-match for charts without replicaCount field; fix: add || true guard to grep pipeline (see sprint reviewNotes)

stories.title: Kaizen: Chart-iterating shell scripts must be validated against all existing platform/charts before passes:true
stories.description: HA-001 failed review twice because scripts/ha-gate.sh used set -euo pipefail with a bare grep pipeline. When processing platform/charts/_globals/ (which has no top-level replicaCount field), grep exited 1, and pipefail killed the script silently. The test plan only covered a synthetic fixture chart — it never ran against the actual chart corpus. This story adds an explicit requirement to the SMART/AC guidelines: any shell script story that iterates platform/charts/ must include 'run against all existing charts in platform/charts/' as an acceptance criterion (not just synthetic fixtures). Update the relevant ceremony docs or story schema notes to enforce this.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md (or equivalent SMART guidance doc) contains text requiring that chart-iterating scripts be tested against all existing platform/charts/ before marking passes:true
The requirement text includes an explicit note about bash set -euo pipefail with grep pipelines: use '|| true' when grep results may be empty
grep -n 'platform/charts' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md (or equivalent) returns at least one match confirming the guidance is present
stories.testPlan: grep -n 'platform/charts' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md and grep -n 'pipefail\||| true' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md — show output verbatim. Both must return matches.
stories.returnedReason: Root cause: shell script stories iterating the chart corpus only required synthetic fixture tests, missing existing charts with no replicaCount field

smart.notes: Specific score 4: target file named as 'scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart.md (or equivalent)' — the exact file to update is ambiguous. Relevant score 4: process-doc kaizen is useful but does not advance the sprint's feature goals.

stories.title: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh: kind cluster provisioner with --help, --cluster-name, and kind create cluster invocation
stories.description: Create cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh. The script must: (1) support --help flag that prints usage including '--cluster-name NAME (default: sovereign-test)'; (2) accept --cluster-name NAME option defaulting to 'sovereign-test'; (3) print 'Bootstrapping kind cluster: $cluster_name' before running; (4) invoke 'kind create cluster --name "$cluster_name"'. Script must pass shellcheck -S error with set -euo pipefail at the top. Also verify that bootstrap/providers/ is not present in the working tree (it should have been removed in RESTRUCTURE-001a).
acceptanceCriteria: grep -q 'kind create cluster' cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0 (script contains the kind invocation)
[ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] exits 0 (bootstrap/providers/ not in working tree)
stories.testPlan: ls cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && bash -n cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && bash cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --help 2>&1 | grep 'cluster-name' && grep 'kind create cluster' cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && [ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] && echo 'all checks passed'; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Specific score 3: description states 'bootstrap/providers/ should have been removed in RESTRUCTURE-001a' but RESTRUCTURE-001a (backlog, passes:false) is the contract-layer story (schema/validate.py) — it does not remove bootstrap/providers/. The justification is factually stale; the directory is already absent. Achievable score 3: dependency on RESTRUCTURE-001a is listed in the dependencies field but that story is in the backlog (not in this sprint, passes:false); sprint runner may block execution. Measurable score 4: all ACs are binary shell commands; minor concern about the bootstrap/providers AC depending on a condition set up outside this sprint.

stories.title: platform/deploy.sh: Helm deployment orchestrator with --help, --chart-dir, --namespace, and helm upgrade --install invocation
stories.description: Create platform/deploy.sh. The script must: (1) support --help flag that prints usage including '--chart-dir DIR' and '--namespace NS'; (2) accept --chart-dir DIR and --namespace NS required options; (3) print 'Deploying chart: $chart_dir to namespace: $namespace' before running; (4) invoke 'helm upgrade --install' for the given chart and namespace. Script must pass shellcheck -S error with set -euo pipefail at the top.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -q 'helm upgrade --install' platform/deploy.sh exits 0 (script contains the helm invocation)
stories.testPlan: ls platform/deploy.sh && shellcheck -S error platform/deploy.sh && bash -n platform/deploy.sh && bash platform/deploy.sh --help 2>&1 | grep 'chart-dir' && grep 'helm upgrade --install' platform/deploy.sh && echo 'all checks passed'; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Achievable score 3: dependency on RESTRUCTURE-001a is listed but that story is in the backlog (not scheduled in this sprint, passes:false); sprint runner may block execution even though the actual platform/deploy.sh work is fully independent. Measurable score 4: ACs are binary but story does not specify behavior when --chart-dir or --namespace flags are missing (error message, usage output).

stories.title: Chaos Mesh PodChaos experiment YAML + README validates PDB prevents simultaneous pod eviction
stories.description: Create test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml: a Chaos Mesh PodChaos experiment targeting a platform service (e.g. grafana namespace, mode: all) to simultaneously terminate all pods. The PDB minAvailable:1 should prevent the last pod from being evicted. Create test/chaos/README.md explaining how to apply the experiment against a kind cluster running Chaos Mesh (TEST-004), what PDB-enforcement behavior to observe, and how to clean up. The YAML is a static test artifact — it does not run in CI. This covers the 'PDB chaos validation' goal from E15.
acceptanceCriteria: python3 -c "import yaml, sys; yaml.safe_load(open(sys.argv[1]).read())" test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0 (valid YAML)
grep 'mode: all' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml exits 0 (targets all replicas to exercise PDB)
test/chaos/README.md exists and grep -i 'PodDisruptionBudget\|PDB' test/chaos/README.md exits 0
stories.testPlan: ls test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml test/chaos/README.md && python3 -c "import yaml, sys; yaml.safe_load(open(sys.argv[1]).read())" test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep 'kind: PodChaos' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep 'mode: all' test/chaos/pdb-validation.yaml && grep -i 'PDB' test/chaos/README.md; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Relevant score 4: static YAML artifact with no CI enforcement — advances documentation and operator runbook but does not programmatically enforce PDB behavior.

stories.title: ArgoCD global.domain injection: cert-manager, crossplane, sealed-secrets, vault, keycloak
stories.description:
Add helm.parameters entry for global.domain to 5 ArgoCD Application manifests: platform/argocd-apps/platform/cert-manager-app.yaml, crossplane-app.yaml, sealed-secrets-app.yaml, vault-app.yaml, keycloak-app.yaml. For each file, add the following block under spec.source.helm (alongside the existing valueFiles entry):
  parameters:
    - name: global.domain
      value: sovereign-autarky.dev
This ensures all 5 charts receive the cluster domain at sync time regardless of their values.yaml default.
acceptanceCriteria: python3 -c "import yaml; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in ['platform/argocd-apps/platform/cert-manager-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/crossplane-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/sealed-secrets-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/vault-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/keycloak-app.yaml']]" exits 0 (all YAML valid)
stories.testPlan: for f in platform/argocd-apps/platform/cert-manager-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/crossplane-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/sealed-secrets-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/vault-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/keycloak-app.yaml; do grep -q 'global.domain' "$f" && echo "PASS: $f" || echo "FAIL: $f"; done; python3 -c "import yaml; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in ['platform/argocd-apps/platform/cert-manager-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/crossplane-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/sealed-secrets-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/vault-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/keycloak-app.yaml']]" && echo 'YAML valid'; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Specific score 4: exact YAML snippet given but does not specify whether to add under an existing 'helm:' block or create one — implementer must infer from file content. Achievable score 4: 5 files x ~4 lines each is near the top of the one-iteration budget.

stories.title: Verify: all 16 targeted ArgoCD app manifests have global.domain injection
stories.description: Verify that all 16 targeted ArgoCD Application manifests contain global.domain after KAIZEN-009a through KAIZEN-009d complete. The 16 targeted files are: cert-manager, crossplane, sealed-secrets, vault, keycloak, gitlab, harbor, argocd-image-updater, loki, tempo, thanos, falco, istio, kiali, opa-gatekeeper, trivy-operator. Files explicitly exempt (domain-agnostic or non-helm): cilium-app.yaml, rook-ceph-app.yaml, prometheus-stack-app.yaml, code-server-app.yaml, sonarqube-app.yaml, reportportal-app.yaml, sovereign-pm-app.yaml, backstage-app.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: Run the corpus check command: 'for f in platform/argocd-apps/platform/cert-manager-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/crossplane-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/sealed-secrets-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/vault-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/keycloak-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/gitlab-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/harbor-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/platform/argocd-image-updater-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/observability/loki-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/observability/tempo-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/observability/thanos-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/security/falco-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/security/istio-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/security/kiali-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/security/opa-gatekeeper-app.yaml platform/argocd-apps/security/trivy-operator-app.yaml; do grep -q global.domain "$f" || { echo "MISSING: $f"; exit 1; }; done && echo ALL_16_PASS' — must print ALL_16_PASS

python3 -c "import yaml; files=['platform/argocd-apps/platform/cert-manager-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/crossplane-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/sealed-secrets-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/vault-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/keycloak-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/gitlab-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/harbor-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/platform/argocd-image-updater-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/observability/loki-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/observability/tempo-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/observability/thanos-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/security/falco-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/security/istio-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/security/kiali-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/security/opa-gatekeeper-app.yaml','platform/argocd-apps/security/trivy-operator-app.yaml']; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in files]; print('ALL_YAML_VALID')" exits 0 and prints ALL_YAML_VALID

stories.title: code-server chart: PersistentVolumeClaim for /home/coder so workspace survives pod restarts
stories.description: Add platform/charts/code-server/templates/pvc.yaml: a PersistentVolumeClaim for the code-server workspace. The storageClass must reference {{ .Values.global.storageClass }} (never hardcoded). Size must be configurable via values.yaml (default 5Gi). Update the Deployment template to add a volumeMount at /home/coder and a corresponding volume referencing the PVC. This ensures that agent workspaces, cloned git repos, and shell history survive pod restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: helm template platform/charts/code-server/ shows a PersistentVolumeClaim with storageClassName: {{ .Values.global.storageClass }}
helm template platform/charts/code-server/ shows a VolumeMount with mountPath: /home/coder
platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml has a persistence.size field — grep 'persistence:' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ && helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep -A3 'PersistentVolumeClaim' && helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep '/home/coder' && grep 'persistence:' platform/charts/code-server/values.yaml; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Measurable score 4: AC2 expects helm template to output the raw Go template expression '{{ .Values.global.storageClass }}' but helm renders templates — the output will contain the resolved value or an empty string, not the literal expression. The AC should grep for the storageClassName key or a known default value (e.g. 'standard').
